name: Black Beans in Spicy Tamarind Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick over and wash the beans. Soak them overnight in cold water.
2. Soak the tamarind pulp in 2 cups hot water in a nonmetallic bowl for at least 1 hour. Squeeze the pulp with fingers, strain it, pressing out as much juice as possible, then discard the residue.
3. Heat oil in a heavy, shallow pan. Add onions and cook until almost reddish brown (10-12 minutes). Stir constantly, scraping the bottom of the pan.
4. Add ginger and garlic, cooking for 5 minutes longer. Then add chile pepper, cumin, and turmeric, stirring briefly to release flavors.
5. Stir in chopped tomatoes; let them cook until they form a sauce and fat separates out.
6. Drain beans and add them to the pan with about 2 cups of water. Cover and simmer until beans are almost done. Check water level occasionally; if beans look dry, add small amounts of hot water.
7. When beans finish cooking, stir in tamarind pulp and cover. Simmer until beans are very tender.
8. Stir in roasted cumin seeds, garam masala, salt, and half the cilantro leaves. Cover pan and let rest at least 10 minutes before reheating and serving garnished with remaining cilantro.
Note: Tamarind is available in cake form at Indian markets. Adapted from Julie Sahni's "Classic Indian Cooking."
